items.title: 'Very short on facts': Electoral Commission's bizarre communications in a crisis

children.content: As trust in their ability to effectively administer an election erodes by the day, so too seemingly does their transparency with the public.

articles.title: Uncounted SA votes in the 'order of dozens', Attorney-General says

children.content: The number of newly revealed uncounted ballot papers from the March state election is believed to be in the "order of dozens", South Australia's Attorney-General says.

image.altText: A person wearing a blue vest with the words Electoral Officer on it among people lining up at a polling booth

articles.title: More uncounted votes discovered in SA a month after election

children.content: More uncounted votes have been discovered in two electorates a month on from the South Australian state election, and while the state's Electoral Commission says the ballots will not harm the "integrity" of the count, they have not said how many have been found.

image.altText: A ballot box during an election.

gallery.altText: A woman holding a ballot walks past other people voting facing away in cardboard booths

thumbnail.altText: A woman holding a ballot walks past other people voting facing away in cardboard booths

items.title: Lack of 24-hour health care in SA prisons questioned

children.content: Advocates call for improved medical resourcing in South Australia's prisons after a coronial inquest into the death of an inmate.

image.altText: Sign at the entrance to low security prison Cadell Training Centre

items.title: Childcare company CEO denies problems at centres a symptom of growing pains

children.content: The head of Edge Early Learning says he is conducting a "whole of business transformation project" after being the subject of compliance action over inadequate supervision and other issues at its childcare centres in Adelaide.

image.altText: A sign saying EDGE in front of a childcare centre

items.title: Stump that created hole in footpath becomes neighbourhood icon

children.content: Adelaide residents have decorated a large tree stump and then the hole it left in a footpath to highlight the local council's delay in removing it.

gallery.altText: A rotten tree stump surrounded by traffic cones, including one topped by a green hat

thumbnail.altText: A rotten tree stump surrounded by traffic cones, including one topped by a green hat

items.title: Woman's life 'changed for the better' after GPs in SA trained to diagnose ADHD

children.content: About 100 South Australian GPs are now qualified to diagnose ADHD and provide treatment, with the hope of that number doubling by the end of the year, with one patient describing her diagnosis through her GP as "life-changing".

image.altText: A person standing in the Adelaide CBD.

items.title: Gift bags and cruise ship fallout part of Adelaide's COP31 considerations

children.content: A heavily redacted feasibility study into Adelaide's capacity to host the COP31 UN climate summit provides a fleeting glimpse into what was under consideration — including politically fraught floating hotels.

image.altText: Adelaide River Torrens rowing

items.title: Tim Picton's accused killer pleads guilty over separate assault

children.content: A man who allegedly killed Labor identity Tim Picton in Perth admits assaulting another man in Kalamunda just days earlier.

image.altText: Brodie Dewar leaves court surrounded by reporters holding microphones.

items.title: Meet the eco-venture turning an invasive pest into dog treats

children.content: Introducing the European carp to Australian waterways is an ecological disaster that continues to worsen, but an Adelaide couple have found a novel use for the pest.

image.altText: Young woman feeds a kelpie a treat

items.title: Farmers fear potential mouse plague as CSIRO warns of high numbers

children.content: As if the cost of diesel and the lack of fertiliser were not enough, South Australia's farmers are also facing the prospect of a mouse plague.

image.altText: a close up of a man's hands holding a live mouse

items.title: SA carer allegedly sexually assaulted woman with disability, police say

children.content: A 55-year-old man from Adelaide's west has been arrested and charged over the alleged sexual assault of a woman with a disability at Glenelg.

image.altText: The South Australia Police logo on a building.

items.title: 'It's just bulldozed through': Native title holders allege cultural sites have been destroyed by mining company

children.content: A First Nations group on South Australia's Yorke Peninsula says a mining company has bulldozed areas of cultural significance and has started development without a formal agreement with Traditional Owners, but the company says it has "continuously engaged" with the group.

image.altText: Two Aboriginal men in black shirts standing alongside each other with a serious expression to the camera

items.title: The artworks we can't stop thinking about from the Adelaide Biennial

children.content: The legacy of nuclear testing, environmental degradation and more among the challenges facing Australia, as explored by artists in the Adelaide Biennial of Australian art.

image.altText: Archie Moore, an Indigenous man, mid-50s, in a cap, holds a heart made of gold in his purple-gloved hands.

items.title: Hospital cancels birthing services again, forcing expectant parents to travel

children.content: Two regional hospitals, 30 minutes' drive apart, struggle to consistently provide routine medical services, forcing repeated patient diversions.

image.altText: A pregnant woman's belly as she holds hands with someone

items.title: As flight options shrink, this regional city is hoping for a return to rail

children.content: Trains stopped coming to Mount Gambier decades ago, but with fewer flights, locals want them back.

image.altText: A man wearing a red and brown striped jumper stands underneath a black sign with white writing which reads "Mount Gambier"

items.title: Five per cent home deposit scheme fuelling price hike, report suggests

children.content: New research from property analytics firm Cotality appears to show that the Home Guarantee Scheme, intended to help first home buyers, may be worsening entry level affordability.

image.altText: A house with a sold sign in the front yard.

items.title: Pacific leaders 'totally blindsided' by declaration of fuel emergency

children.content: Confusion and some frustration remains after a key Pacific leader announced an emergency declaration had been agreed to over fuel, despite other leaders not knowing about it.

image.altText: A man in a suit rests his hand on his chest, standing in front of Solomon Island and China flags.

items.title: Australian billionaire's waste-to-energy plan labelled 'waste colonialism'

children.content: The controversial project would see waste burned to create energy in Fiji to reduce the country's reliance on fossil fuels, but locals and environmentalists are not happy.

image.altText: A deep-water port with large cranes, a cargo ship and shipping containers sits in front of a large building with two chimneys

items.title: 'Ghosted': Musicians claim gig booker owes thousands of dollars

children.content: A triple j hack investigation uncovers allegations that a booking agency has failed to pay more than 20 Australian bands and artists.

image.altText: Man in white shirt and black cap plays guitar and sings into microphone.

items.title: Police officer given 'golden pass' on rape allegations, victim says

children.content: When Tasmania Police misidentified Laura as a family violence perpetrator, she had no idea she'd have to fight for them to hold her abuser — a serving officer — to account. Now, she's fighting for change.

gallery.altText: A police officer's torso showing a body cam

thumbnail.altText: A police officer's torso showing a body cam

items.title: EU backs $148bn loan for Ukraine after pipeline spat resolved

children.content: A bitter feud over an oil pipeline pitted Hungary against Ukraine and stalled the vitally needed funds for Kyiv.

image.altText: Volodymr Zelenskyy gestures as he sits in front of the Ukrainian flag.

items.title: 70yo 'duped' into importing heroin to Sydney in fold-out camping bed jailed

children.content: Barry James Calverley's lawyers argued he was an innocent drug mule who naively and gullibly trusted a story about why he needed to bring the bed to Australia.

image.altText: Barry Calverley interviewed by police.

items.title: More than a third of Australians are seeking food relief for the first time

children.content: About one in three people are accessing food relief services for the first time, according to a new report from Food charity OzHarvest.

gallery.altText: A group of people wearing black t-shirts preparing food in a kitchen.

thumbnail.altText: A group of people wearing black t-shirts preparing food in a kitchen.

items.title: ICC judges reject bid to release former Philippines president Rodrigo Duterte

children.content: Earlier hearings heard Rodrigo Duterte was allegedly responsible for thousands of deaths during his war on drugs.

image.altText: Former Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte

items.title: 'Overpowered' electric motorbikes could still be sold under proposed e-bike laws

children.content: Cycling advocates say as long as the electric dirt bikes are sold for use on private land, they won't have to meet the stringent requirements of regular e-bikes.

image.altText: An action shot of an e-bike rider performing a wheelie

items.title: Liberty jobs secure for now, but what happens when the money runs out?

children.content: Three weeks of wages have been guaranteed for Liberty Bell Bay smelter workers, but with no buyer locked in, questions remain about what happens next.

image.altText: men and women in orange high vis outside a blue logo and a rusted sculpture

items.title: 101yo Bombing of Darwin veteran wants Australians to know what unfolded

children.content: At 101 years old, Bombing of Darwin survivor Terrance Byrne wants Australians to know exactly what unfolded during the World War II attack on Darwin — still the single largest foreign attack on Australian soil.

image.altText: An older man sitting in front of table of framed black and white pictures.

items.title: 50 years of Ramones — the album nobody bought but everyone copied

children.content: Fed up with where rock 'n' roll was headed, these four New Yorkers changed the music world by playing sweet pop hooks at breakneck speed.

gallery.altText: Four members of Ramones stand outdoors in 1970s-style jeans and graphic T‑shirts, posing in front of flowering bushes

thumbnail.altText: Four members of Ramones stand outdoors in 1970s-style jeans and graphic T‑shirts, posing in front of flowering bushes

items.title: 'Lost everything': New Zealand's capital cleans up as rain pushes north

children.content: Police have found the body of a man as floodwaters recede in New Zealand's capital and the clean-up begins.

image.altText: People kneel into gutters to collect rocks and soil dislodged in heavy rain

items.title: Australia ready for AUKUS subs as China threat looms, US commander says

children.content: The US's Indo-Pacific commander praises work to upgrade Australia's AUKUS base, but says he needs more naval firepower in the region as China rapidly expands its military.

image.altText: Admiral Samuel Paparo sits at a desk and speaks into a microphone with uniformed personnel behind him.

items.title: Gaza conditions 'catastrophic' as report finds $US71b needed to rebuild

children.content: More than $US71 billion will be needed over the next 10 years for recovery and reconstruction in Gaza, according to a new report by the European Union and the United Nations.

image.altText: Palestinian woman in a headscarf looks out a from a window of a tent set up inside her war-damaged home.

items.title: 'Spectacularly ill-advised': Energy giants condemn gas tax

children.content: An inquiry into the taxation of gas resources has heard from proponents for a tax, who believe Australians are being robbed of their fair share of wealth from the nation's natural resources, and opponents, who argue touching tax settings would spook investors.

image.altText: A woman wearing glasses leans earnestly towards a microphone.

items.title: Police 'not fully prepared' for violent scene they were called to, NT inquest hears

children.content: An inquest hears evidence from a senior police officer who ran into a room to disarm a mother who was self-harming inside a Darwin crisis accommodation facility, describing the incident as "one of the worst days" of her policing career.

image.altText: A police car outside the Darwin Local Court.

items.title: 'It's been horrible': Your experiences with family estrangement

children.content: Our recent stories about the grief of family estrangement resonated strongly. Here's how some Australians have been affected by it.

image.altText: An illustration of a woman sitting on a park bench looking update with her hands on her head

items.title: Iran's meme factory is using 'the weapons of their enemy' to embarrass Trump

children.content: AI-generated Lego memes criticising the conflict in the Middle East and mocking Donald Trump are attracting a large audience online as a new front opens in the Iran war.

image.altText: AI-generated image of Donald Trump animated in a lego-style with a terrified expression.

gallery.altText: A close up of a Lego figure that looks like Trump sweating and holding up a sign.

thumbnail.altText: A close up of a Lego figure that looks like Trump sweating and holding up a sign.

avatar.alt: A close up photo of a brunette woman wearing glasses.
